title: Beam manipulation system
abstract: A beam director, typically comprises a first mirror rotating about a longitudinal axis, with a reflective surface at an acute angle to the longitudinal axis, which enables a laser beam to be transmitted along the longitudinal axis and redirected onto a work surface, which is typically perpendicular to the longitudinal axis. A second stationary arcuate mirror segment may be used to reflect the beam along an arcuate path on the work surface. Previous beam director systems can be improved or simplified by: 1) elimination of the second mirror with a 90Â° reflection to the work surface; 2) fixing the Tangent factor when drawing/rendering/sintering/cutting using f-theta like lens; and 3) fixing the Tangent factor by controlling the amount and/or the duration of energy.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A beam director comprising: a light source for generating a beam of light; a reflector configured to receive the beam of light from the light source along a rotational axis of the reflector for reflecting the beam onto a work surface at a constant angle to the rotational axis; and an actuator for rotating the reflector about the rotational axis; wherein the rotational axis is substantially parallel to the work surface; and wherein the reflector is capable of receiving the beam of light along the rotational axis, and reflecting the beam of light onto the work surface at a plurality of different angular positions of the reflector following a path relative to the work surface and tracing out a line perpendicular to the rotational axis on the work surface. 2. The beam director according to claim 1 , further comprising: a controller for controlling the amount of energy delivered by the light source to a work surface as the angular position of the reflector changes to at least partially compensate for a loss of energy due to a change in distance and orientation to the work surface as the reflector rotates. 3. The beam director according to claim 2, wherein the controller is capable of increasing an amount of time the reflector directs the beam on a desired position on the work surface, as the angular position of the reflector changes to at least partially compensate for the reduction in energy. 4. The beam director according to claim 2, wherein the controller is capable of increasing an amount of power delivered by the laser on a desired position on the work surface, as the angular position of the reflector changes to compensate for the reduction in energy. 5. The beam director according to claim 2, wherein the controller is capable of both increasing the amount of power delivered by the laser on a desired position on the work surface, and increasing an amount of time the reflector directs the beam on a 

 desired position on the work surface, as the angular position of the reflector changes to compensate for the reduction in energy. 6. The beam director according to claim 1 , wherein the reflector comprises a focussing mirror; and further comprising a first distance adjuster for adjusting a distance between the reflector and the light source. 7. The beam director according to claim 1 , wherein the reflector comprises an off axis parabolic type. 8. The beam director according to claim 1 , further comprising a focussing lens disposed between the reflector and the work surface for equalizing a distance between positions on the work surface for equal changes in angular position of the reflector. 9. The beam director according to claim 1 , further comprising an F-theta correction lens. 10. A print head for a three dimensional printer comprising the beam director of claim 1 . 1 1 . A method of directing a beam towards a work surface with a beam director, the method comprising: generating a beam of light with a light source; rotating a reflector about a rotational axis with an actuator; directing the beam from the light source towards the reflector along the rotational axis of the reflector; reflecting the beam with the reflector at an angle to the rotational axis towards the work surface; whereby the reflector receives the beam of light along the rotational axis, which is parallel to the work surface, and reflects the beam of light onto the work surface at a 

 plurality of different angular positions of the reflector following a path relative to the work surface and tracing out a line perpendicular to the rotational axis on the work surface 12. The method according to claim 1 1 , further comprising: controlling the amount of energy delivered by the light source to the work surface as the angular position of the reflector changes to compensate for a loss of energy due to a change in distance and orientation to the work surface as the reflector rotates; so that when the beam is activated and the actuator rotates the reflector; the beam strikes the the work surface, and the beam follows a path relative to the work surface and traces out a line on the work surface. 13. The method according to claim 12, wherein the controlling step comprises increasing an amount of time the reflector directs the beam on a desired position on the work surface, as the angular position of the reflector changes to compensate for the reduction in energy. 14. The method according to claim 12, wherein the controlling step comprises increasing an amount of power delivered by the laser on a desired position on the work surface, as the angular position of the reflector changes to compensate for the reduction in energy. 15. The method according to claim 12, wherein the controlling step comprises both increasing the amount of power delivered by the laser on a desired position on the work surface, and increasing an amount of time the reflector directs the beam on a desired position on the work surface, as the angular position of the reflector changes to compensate for the reduction in energy 16. The method according to claim 1 1 , further comprising adjusting the distance between the laser and the reflector to focus the beam of light. 17. The method according to claim 1 1 , wherein the reflector comprises an off axis parabolic type. 18. The method according to claim 1 1 , further comprising disposing a correcting lens disposed between the reflector and the work surface for equalizing a distance between positions on the work surface for equal changes in angular position of the reflector. 19. The method according to claim 18, wherein the correcting lens comprises an F-theta lens. 20. The method of claim 1 1 further includes using the beam director as a print head for a three dimensional printer.
